mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero (0.12%) in mid-2015, a continued flat-to-declining effective funds rate trend signals persistent accommodative monetary policy, lowering the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive FEDFUNDS trend periods do not show statistically higher mean forward returns than negative-trend periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below a 5.3% threshold (approaching pre-crisis full-employment levels from the current 5.5%), markets price in accelerated Fed liftoff timing, causing a short-term negative repricing of equities over the next 5 trading days as rate-hike risk premia rise.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5% as of t-1mo, near multi-year lows in 2015
regime_hint: labor market tightening approaching full employment
failure_condition: Following UNRATE crossings below 5.3%, SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, invalidating the negative-repricing mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales, currently 444,926M) and HOUST (housing starts, currently 1,135K units) indicates simultaneous strength in consumer spending and residential investment, the two largest components of final demand, which compounds into sustained equity gains over a 60-day horizon beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS+HOUST acceleration signal fails to outperform a linear combination of the two individual single-series signals in out-of-sample Rank IC by any margin over a 3-year rolling window, or produces Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where CPIAUCSL inflation remains subdued (235.982, well below Fed's implicit path) while FEDFUNDS sits near zero, the market interprets low inflation prints as delaying policy normalization, supporting risk asset valuations; this positive relationship between low/falling CPI momentum and forward equity returns reverses once inflation regime shifts toward the Fed's 2% target trajectory.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL 235.982 as of t-2mo with FEDFUNDS near zero-bound
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth rises above 2.5% and forward SPX returns remain positive rather than turning negative, or if the low-inflation-supports-equities relationship shows Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO's 3-month annualized growth rate below zero, signaling contraction in industrial output (index at 105.10 as of t-1mo), foreshadows deteriorating corporate earnings for goods-producing sectors and precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as markets reprice cyclical earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following INDPRO contraction triggers are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO growth and forward returns is below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls, currently 141,679K) reflects continued labor market expansion and consumer income growth, which supports near-term risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as payroll data releases are digested by markets.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS month-over-month growth periods fail to show significantly higher mean 5-day forward SPX returns than negative-growth periods (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of falling UNRATE (labor tightening) combined with a still-near-zero FEDFUNDS rate creates a Phillips-curve-style tension: markets price rising probability of imminent rate liftoff disproportionately when both labor tightness and policy accommodation coexist, generating a nonlinear negative equity return reaction over 20 trading days that neither series alone predicts.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines > 0.2pp over 3 months while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE 5.5% and FEDFUNDS 0.12% as of decision date, pre-liftoff period
regime_hint: late zero-lower-bound period ahead of anticipated policy liftoff
failure_condition: The interaction signal's Rank IC does not exceed the maximum of the two individual single-series Rank ICs over any 2-year rolling window, or triggered instances show positive forward returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-cycle upswing regime, HOUST crossing above its trailing 12-month moving average signals renewed residential investment momentum that historically leads broader economic activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon; this relationship is specific to upswing regimes and does not hold during housing downturns or oversupply corrections.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its trailing 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,135K units as of t-2mo
failure_condition: During confirmed housing upswing regimes, SPX 60-day forward returns following HOUST moving-average crossings are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
